title: 5 free analyses experiment — run the positioning-pivot validation (per CLAUDE.md and docs/internal/product-positioning-2026-04-13.md). Goal: validate the '$100-500 musicologist you can't afford' frame by offering 5 free analyses to early users and measuring whether they convert / refer / produce shareable output. Out-of-scope: any landing-page copy in the old $29-99 frame (paused per 2026-04-14). Interactive-only — requires Ray's outreach judgment, real-user conversations, and positioning calls.
interactive_only_reason: Positioning + pricing validation with real early users. Needs Ray's outreach voice, judgment on who to approach, and live conversation follow-up. No bot-safe slice.

title: Reference-DB per-track fallback populates — when catalogdna's bot (launched via cdna-XXX trigger) can't find a clean playlist_url for a target album in bot_tasks.md populate work, take over the song-by-song workaround: (a) search YouTube for each track individually, (b) record per-track video IDs, (c) invoke the track-level downloader with those explicit URLs, (d) verify + commit. Judgment-heavy — the bot handles the common playlist-URL case; Ray handles the per-track fallback when the playlist path is flaky.
interactive_only_reason: Per-track URL search + manual sanity check on which videos match the canonical album sequence is judgment work. Bot handles the common case (cdna-004); Ray handles the fallback.

title: Dislikes-as-first-class-input design spec (per CLAUDE.md §'Product Direction: Dislikes as First-Class Input' and docs/internal/product-philosophy-dislikes-as-input.md). Not a current build target — this is the design-spec work that precedes build. Produce: (a) UI placement for the 'dislikes' input alongside existing influences field, (b) analytical model for how dislikes data feeds into the interpret layer, (c) how dislikes affect fingerprint narrative. Interactive-only — core product direction.
interactive_only_reason: Product-direction design spec. Taste work — which signal to surface, how to narrate, how it interacts with the honest-findings principle.

title: GS cycle trigger — permission for the dispatcher to launch catalogdna's existing autonomous bot. This task does NOT describe specific work; catalogdna's bot (run_bot.sh + CLAUDE-AUTONOMOUS.md) has its own native task queue in bot_tasks.md and picks from that. When GS's dispatcher picks this trigger, it runs `bash run_bot.sh ${cycle_budget_minutes}`, catalogdna's bot reads bot_tasks.md, does whatever's highest priority there (populate, test coverage, bug fix, audit, etc.), commits to bot/work, and exits. GS then runs its verification gate + reviewer on whatever diff landed. Ray maintains bot_tasks.md independently — that's where specific populate entries, test-coverage entries, etc. live. Re-add cdna-XXX triggers (cdna-005, cdna-006, ...) whenever Ray wants GS to kick off catalogdna's bot opportunistically. Empty bot_tasks.md → empty-diff cycle → verified_weak → task closes cleanly.

PDF-export + Obsidian-wiki tier split — 2026-04-21 product-direction idea from Ray (captured during a walk). Current default output is the full Obsidian wiki. New direction: default output becomes an electronic PDF styled AS IF it were an Obsidian wiki (hyperlinked, branching, graph-feel visual language) — and the actual editable Obsidian wiki graduates to a higher-paid tier. Rationale: (a) more control over the PDF output — we own rendering, users don't edit around us; (b) a Claude Design pass can produce the PDF visual aesthetic cleanly (per CLAUDE.local.md §Skills-first tool integration — save a SKILL.md to catalogdna/.claude/skills/ once the aesthetic lands); (c) free/basic tier delivers real value without giving away the richer artifact; (d) monetization tier structure emerges naturally from format difference rather than feature-gate fiction. Existing tool options worth surveying: react-pdf skill at ~/.claude/skills/react-pdf/ (already proven via VC's manual generation); devforge has PDF tools per Ray mention; other OSS PDF-from-markdown generators possibly worth a look. Scope for this task (design spec, not build): (1) decide default-tier format (PDF-only vs PDF-+-basic-wiki-vs-something-else), (2) scope the Claude Design pass on the PDF aesthetic — pick a starter from awesome-claude-design that evokes the Obsidian-wiki feel (dark + typographic + graph-referential — Raycast, The Verge, or a custom brief are plausible vibes), (3) spec the tier gating (what content lives where, how the upgrade path works, pricing signal), (4) scope build effort against current catalogdna architecture + where render code lives. Does NOT commit to building — this is direction capture + spec. Interactive — product-tier direction is taste + business judgment.
interactive_only_reason: Product-tier direction + monetization design. Taste call on where the free/paid line draws; format decisions interact with current render architecture and cannot be automated away.
